danger_signals.visual_form: distinctive animated creature sprites with aggressive posturing; visual distinctiveness from player character
armed bipedal alien forms with laser weaponry; differentiated silhouette from Lester
danger_signals.meaning: organized hostile faction; lethal ranged threat

danger_signals.visual_form: contextual stage elements (water, energy fields, collapsing structures) rendered in scene-specific visual language
projectile trails with distinct color (likely yellow/red in vector style); visible on-screen weapon discharge
visual barriers created by enemy laser fire; distinguishable from standard terrain
danger_signals.meaning: tactical obstacle; requires specific tool response (charged shot)

safety_signals.visual_form: distinct humanoid alien character model accompanying Lester; recognizable friendly silhouette
safety_signals.meaning: cooperative narrative state; tactile gameplay support for puzzle-solving and narrative progression

safety_signals.visual_form: environmental elements with distinct animation states (cages that rock, breakable walls, interactive platforms)
safety_signals.meaning: safe mechanical opportunity; indicates solvable challenge rather than direct combat threat

safety_signals.visual_form: spatial staging that allows level restart without narrative penalty; implicit visual spacing suggests safe zone completion
weapon pickup animation; visible gun asset in Lester's hand; three distinct firing modes visible through gameplay
safety_signals.meaning: power-up state transition; shifts from defenseless to combat-capable world-state

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: armed soldiers with laser weaponry; organized prison-camp infrastructure visible in level design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: subterranean prison complex; structured architecture; technological elements (force fields, energy barriers); organized spatial hierarchy suggesting control of territory

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: distinct from soldier faction through animation and positioning; captive status indicated visually before escape sequence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: prison camp detainee; later cooperative positioning; rescue narrative placement

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: wild creature designs; aggressive behavioral animation; non-organized threat patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: barren alien planet surface; cave systems; distributed throughout uncontrolled territories

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: teleportation to barren alien landscape; visual contrast between high-tech laboratory and hostile natural environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lester unarmed and defenseless; limited kick attack capability; evasion-focused movement patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: subterranean prison architecture; confined spaces; visible guard presence; Buddy introduced as fellow captive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: organized enemy patrol patterns; restricted movement; puzzle-based escape mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: laser pistol visual asset acquired; laser fire visible on-screen; force field generation by enemies becomes visible threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: combat stance changes; player can engage enemies directly; three-mode weapon system displayed through gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: transition to natural cavern environment; mix of indigenous fauna and soldier presence; environmental puzzle elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: hybrid threat profile; creature encounters mixed with organized military opposition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: vertical architecture; elevated visual perspective shifts; climactic encounter staging
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lester severely wounded state indicated visually; Buddy support mechanics intensified; escape sequence initiated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: dragon-like creature visual asset; vertical ascent animation; departure from alien world geography
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: cooperative animation between Lester and Buddy; flight sequence; narrative closure visual cue

environmental_storytelling.example: subterranean prison complex visual design communicates organized alien captor control; structured geometry vs. natural cave chaos indicates territory dominance
indigenous fauna rendered distinctly from humanoid soldiers; visual morphology signals 'animal hazard' vs. 'military opponent' without text
laser pistol pickup triggers visible gameplay mode changes (three-fire-mode system); visual manifestation of narrative and mechanical power progression
journey from barren surface → prison camp → caves → tower → escape charts story through environmental location hierarchy and visual contrast

environmental_storytelling.technique: Animation and silhouette for character relationship
environmental_storytelling.example: Buddy's distinct alien silhouette and animations visually differentiate from both enemies and Lester; cooperative animation sequences convey alliance without dialogue
environmental puzzles (rocking cages, breakable walls) become visible as safe-mechanic opportunities through distinct animation responses vs. insta-kill contact hazards

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: evasion-focused movement; limited kick attacks; visible vulnerability posture; restricted combat stance
combat-ready stance animation; active laser firing; tactical positioning for force-field blocking; three-mode weapon use patterns
slowed movement animation; collapse pose; dependency on Buddy for support and escape
captive positioning; collaborative puzzle-solving animation; prisoner silhouette
flanking Lester during travel; aggressive action against captors; rescue and support mechanics; flight escape animation
organized movement patterns; laser weapon discharge with three-mode variants (standard, force field, charged); tactical positioning in structured environments
wild, non-coordinated attack patterns; creature-specific animation sets; distributed threat presence in unstructured terrain
static or predictable animation; one-hit-kill contact trigger; visual distinctiveness from safe passage routes

notes: Another World communicates world-state almost entirely through visual and behavioral manifestation, with minimal HUD and no on-screen text or character dialogue (all aliens speak unintelligible language). The game's cinematic vector-art style creates strong visual taxonomy: player character, allied alien, hostile soldiers, wild fauna, and environmental hazards are all distinguishable through silhouette, animation, and contextual positioning. Danger is communicated through: creature presence, soldier engagement, environmental geometry, and laser fire visibility. Safety signals rely on puzzle-object framing, checkpoint positioning, and Buddy's collaborative stance. The narrative arc (barren surface → imprisonment → escape → tower → flight) unfolds through location progression rather than exposition. The three-mode laser system (standard / force field / charged shot) is visible through on-screen weapon effects and enemy mirror-use, teaching mechanics without tutorial text. One-hit-kill collision rules create tension through visual proximity cues rather than health bars. Faction identity emerges from architecture (prison complex = organized captor control) and creature design taxonomy rather than explicit symbology.
